text:
BOTTOM LINE — Quiet grind, no conviction, energy faded. SPY +0.43% with volume at 0.69x average and VIX falling to 15.0 signals a low-participation rally — dealers bought gamma near 753 flip while staples and materials led on no catalyst, a classic rangebound tape with no breakout conviction.

INDICES — VIX 15.0, down. SPY +0.43%, DIA +0.30%, QQQ +0.31%, IWM -0.42%, SOXX -0.06%. Large > small by 0.37pp. Most notable cross-asset move: GLD -0.31% falling alongside USO -0.28% — both underperforming equities.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 0.7x, QQQ 0.7x — both declining. Volume down + price up = low-conviction rally. XLC 0.9x gaining attention while XLK 0.5x being ignored, confirming rotation out of tech into staples and comms.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLB Materials +1.25%, XLP Staples +1.11%, XLC Comms +1.02%, XLU Utilities +0.62%, XLRE Real Estate +0.50%, XLE Energy +0.47%, XLI Industrials +0.45%, XLY Discretionary +0.33%, XLF Financials +0.31%, XLK Technology +0.23%, XLV Healthcare -0.82%. Top Industries: OIH +1.53%, URA +1.46%. Bottom Industries: XBI -3.20%, WGMI -2.95%. Money rotating out of healthcare and biotech into materials and staples — broad risk-on but defensive-leaning. Rotation state: rangebound chop.

POSITIONING — QQQ PCR_vol 1.33 elevated — heavy protection demand on Nasdaq. SPY skew +3.0% bullish, GEX flip at 753, spot near maxpain 748 — dealer hedging dampens downside. VIX at 15.0 below its 20-day MA of 16.9 — complacency in vol.

CROSS-ASSET — Gold -0.31% and oil -0.28% both falling while SPY rises — this divergence suggests the equity rally is liquidity-driven, not economic optimism.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 68 Greed. Top movers: META +5.97% (Mag 7) on $10B Canada data center news, UTES +0.64% (utilities) on vol spike. Watch tomorrow: META — vol 2.1x, accumulation signal; XBI — -3.20% distribution, possible biotech trend break; IWM — -0.42%, below 200-day MA if that threshold applies.
